text.title: AI Agent Guardrails Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Agent Guardrails Pack application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each guardrail MUST be authored once in a neutral YAML rule and translated per agent (Copilot system prompt, Cursor rules, Claude Code allowed-tools). Per-agent forks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Per-agent forks drift; a single source of truth keeps the legal/security signoff transitive across agents.

rule.statement: Every guardrail MUST have a corresponding lint rule that fires on the same code pattern. Guardrails without lint parity are rejected.
rule.rationale: Agents are best-effort; lint is enforcement; both layers must agree or the agent learns to bypass the unenforced rule.

rule.statement: Guardrails MUST forbid echoing PII (emails, SSNs, phone numbers, payment card numbers) into agent prompts or outputs. The forbid rule MUST emit detected violations with a redacted snippet.
rule.rationale: PII echo into agent context is a regulatory breach (GDPR Art 32, HIPAA 164.502) and a leading cause of in-IDE compliance incidents.

rule.statement: Guardrails MUST forbid paste of file contents matching secret patterns (private keys, API tokens, .env files). Detected paste blocks the agent action.
rule.rationale: Once a secret enters agent context it must be rotated; the guardrail prevents the trigger rather than chasing the incident.

rule.statement: The pack MUST carry signoff from a named security or compliance reviewer. Unsigned packs MUST NOT be installed in client repos.
rule.rationale: Without signoff the pack is engineering opinion; with signoff it is a control the client can defend to their own auditor.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
